reasoning: As instructed, I used the statistical trend analysis from FRED as my primary anchor, which provided a combined estimate of P(>1.305) = 0.32. The recent spot value (1.3051) is near a local peak compared to the linear trend line (which forecasts 1.295 for April 20). Over time, foreign exchange rates behave approximately as a random walk, so as the forecast horizon extends, the probability of exceeding the threshold moves toward 0.50 due to an increase in variance outweighing the short-term mean-reversion or trending effects. Therefore, I start with 0.32 for the near-term date (April 20) and steadily increase the probabilities for subsequent dates (May 13, July 12, Oct 10) to reflect growing uncertainty, approaching a near coin flip for the 6-month horizon.
